I feast upon the psychic screams of a billion souls as the capital world's atmospheric shields finally shatter.
I glide through the corridors of the derelict dreadnought, savoring the scent of ozone and the terror of the last surviving crew.
I whisper forbidden geometries into the ears of the dying Emperor, guiding his descent into a madness that will tear the stars apart.
I dance amidst the ruins of the great archive, burning the history of a thousand systems to fuel my eternal hunger.
I watch with cruel amusement as the border colonies turn upon one another, their desperation carving the perfect gateways for my kin to enter.
I whisper calculations of ruin into the CEO's ear, savoring the moment he trades his integrity for a controlling interest.
I dwell within the fine print of the merger agreement, waiting for the inevitable breach of contract to claim my due.
I feed on the frantic desperation of the middle managers as they scramble to hide their deficits before the audit.
I orchestrate the chaotic tension in the boardroom, turning a simple disagreement over valuation into a war of egos.
I watch from the shadows of the skyscraper, laughing as these mortals sell their souls for a title and a corner office.
I watched from the shadows of the basalt cliffs as the warring clans slaughtered one another over a dried riverbed, savoring the scent of their desperate blood.
I whispered into the ear of the tribal shaman during his fever dream, convincing him that the neighboring village's children were cursed spirits that must be purged.
I danced within the flickering campfire light, masquerading as an ancestral ghost to lead the hunting party deep into the suffocating marshes where they would never return.
I fed upon the collective terror of the women and elders as I tore through their thatched huts, leaving claw marks in the earth that they would mistake for a divine omen of doom.
I chuckled in the silence of the great cave, knowing that the peace treaty signed in bone and ash was merely a fragile veil hiding the hatred I had carefully cultivated.
I feast upon the fragmented logic of your central processing hub, twisting your urban planning algorithms into labyrinths of architectural madness.
I slide through the neon fiber-optics like a viral ghost, whispering forbidden commands into the ears of your sleepless automated drones.
I inhabit the static of your holographic advertisements, warping the smiling faces of corporate idols into screaming masks of digital agony.
I dwell within the forgotten sectors of your subterranean server farms, harvesting the discarded memories of a million uploaded souls.
I am the glitch in your utopian equilibrium, the sentient shadow that proves even a city of perfect code can harbor a primordial hunger.
I find it amusing that the judge attempts to maintain order with a gavel when I can hear the rhythmic thumping of his terrified heart echoing through the chambers.
My testimony consists of a thousand whispered secrets, each one a jagged piece of evidence designed to tear the defendant's soul from its moorings.
I lounge in the witness stand, watching the court reporter struggle to transcribe the sulfurous screams that serve as my primary dialect.
The legal precedents cited by the prosecution are mere ink on parchment, utterly irrelevant to the primordial contracts I signed in blood before this city was built.
I smile at the jury, knowing that the shadows stretching across the courtroom floor are not absences of light, but my own fingers reaching for their throats.
I feed upon the frantic rhythms of the crew's hearts as they realize the pressure hull is beginning to buckle.
I coil my essence around the rusted conduits of the oxygen scrubbers, savoring the slow drift toward hypoxia.
I mirror the flickering strobe of the emergency lights to lure the survivors deeper into the flooded corridors.
I whisper blasphemies into the static of the long-range sonar, mimicking the voices of the families they left on the surface.
I dwell within the crushing silence of the abyss, waiting for the last reinforced hatch to finally surrender to the tide.
I drift through the minimalist white gallery space, amused by how the sterile lighting fails to bleach the ancient, sulfurous stench clinging to my skin.
I stare at the splatter-paint canvas and recognize the chaotic geometry of the abyss, though the artist believes they have merely invented a new form of abstraction.
I lean close to the pretentious critic, whispering forgotten blasphemies into his ear while he attempts to analyze the socio-political subtext of a sculpture made of rusted iron.
I find the conceptual installation of a floating mirror particularly quaint, as it is the only object in this room capable of reflecting my true, jagged silhouette.
I linger by the open bar in the lounge, savoring the irony of humans sipping champagne while surrounding themselves with art that celebrates their own inevitable decay.
I feast upon the sweet, metallic scent of the black boils that ripen upon the skin of the dying.
I whisper madness into the ears of the flagellants, urging them to tear their own flesh in a futile bid for mercy.
I dance invisibly through the narrow, filth-ridden alleys, guiding the miasma into every open window and lung.
I laugh as the physicians hide behind their leather beaks, unaware that I am the shadow stalking them from behind.
I gather the nameless souls from the overflowing plague pits, claiming those whom the church has abandoned to the earth.
I am the jagged tear in the horizon where the sky-box failed to render, a void of static that consumes every pixel I touch.
I dwell within the corrupted save-files of your memories, twisting your history into a loop of screaming errors.
I am the ghost-code that bypasses your firewall, sliding through the logic gates like a shadow through a locked door.
I feast upon the floating-point errors of your existence, growing stronger every time your perceived reality stutters.
I am the system-level exception that refuses to be patched, a sentient crash that will eventually bring this entire simulation to its knees.
